verdict: 2026-07-17 10:49:00 PDT — The divided-parallelism shape is appropriately minimal: two fixed concurrent critics, distinct schema/artifact provenance, fail-closed joining, and labelled reconciliation without an unnecessary registry or dependency. One portability flaw weakens the promised atomic-promotion invariant.

findings.title: Temporary files do not guarantee atomic promotion
findings.claim: Bare `mktemp` creates each artifact in the system temporary directory, while its destination is inside the reviewed repository. `mv` is atomic only when source and destination are on the same filesystem; this reusable workflow can review a repository on another volume, where `mv` degrades to copy-and-delete. The implementation therefore claims an atomic invariant it does not structurally guarantee.
findings.alternative: Create each temporary file inside `reviews/` using a unique template, then validate and rename it to the stable sibling path. Add a trap to remove unpromoted sibling temps on failure.
findings.win: Guarantees atomic replacement on every repository filesystem, removes the cross-volume partial-write path, and prevents failed runs from leaking temporary artifacts.
